findings.title: Unknown Data and Transmission State on Session Start
findings.summary: Simulator starts with Unknown data/transmission state.

findings.title: Intent Action Contradicts Underlying AI State
findings.summary: Current Intent action "BUY_CALL" contradicts underlying AI "WAIT" state resulting from model failure.

findings.title: Session Starts in Warmup State
findings.summary: Simulator starts in warmup mode as expected on the first tick, with intent set to WAIT.

findings.title: Execution Paused Due to Transmission Instability
findings.summary: Trader behavior demonstrates an explicit pause in execution decision-making despite a perceived 'perfect structural setup' for a PUT due to recurring TRANSMISSION_DELAYED and TRANSMISSION_PENDING states, prioritizing data integrity over execution.

findings.title: Trader Defines Explicit Risk Invalidation Point on Entry
findings.summary: Upon executing BUY_CALL entry at tick 28, the trader explicitly defined a logical risk management invalidation point at 748.24, approximately ~0.34 below the SPY entry spot, confirming active risk containment.

findings.title: Position Exited Successfully per Explicit Risk Invalidation
findings.summary: The system successfully executed the defined risk management strategy, exiting the long CALL position via SPOT_INVALIDATION at an exitSpot of 748.04, having breached the predetermined invalidation level of 748.24.

findings.title: Model Approves Re-Entry, Trader Renews CALL Thesis
findings.summary: Model diagnostics confirm reentry is allowed 20 ticks after the prior invalidation. The trader has renewed the CALL thesis, approving a BUY_CALL intent at tick 57 based on a diagnosed "fresh multi-tick leg" and "continuing structure," demonstrating a persistent bullish conviction.

findings.title: Re-Entry Blocked by Repeated GEX_VELOCITY Constraint
findings.summary: The model has explicitly blocked re-entry into the CALL thesis due to a "repeated GEX_VELOCITY" constraint, causing the intent to flip from BUY_CALL (tick 70) to PREPARE_CALL with a blocker, despite the trader's stated desire to act on a perceived localized target.

findings.title: Canonical GEX Block Overridden by Local Frontier Evidence
findings.summary: The GEX_VELOCITY re-entry blocker reported in prior ticks is successfully overridden at tick 87. The model approved new BUY_CALL intent based on evidence of a 'fresh local/session frontier' and 'cleared prior attempt extreme,' overriding established structural friction.

findings.title: Hold Intent and Position Management Factors Persist After Position Exit
findings.summary: The system successfully exited the CALL position at 11:05 AM for a +29.2% profit. However, in the subsequent tick (97), the 'position' data is correctly null, but the 'intent' action remains HOLD with supporting factors related to managing the now closed position, indicating a lag in intent state update.

findings.title: Diagonal Re-Entry Blocker Overridden by Breakout Evidence
findings.summary: A re-entry blocker for 'repeated ACCEL' (acceleration) is successfully overridden by the model at tick 117. New 'BUY_CALL' intent is approved based on the stated evidence of 'cleared prior attempt extreme,' overriding established diagonal friction.

findings.title: Immediate -25% Contract Drawdown on Canonical Entry
findings.summary: Immediately after entering the canonical CALL fill at tick 127 at a price of 0.20, the positional review in tick 128 reports the current contract price as 0.15, marking an immediate -25.0% drawdown, suggesting severe slippage or pricing discontinuity.

findings.title: Trader Rationale Conflicts with Objective Price Action During mounting Drawdown
findings.summary: The trader maintains a HOLD mindset at 11:53 AM, reasoning 'price acceptance above 750.00' and 'breakout context', yet the actual current spot price is 749.87 and contract drawdown has reached -30%. This suggests a critical disconnect between the subjective rationale for holding and objective price action criteria.

findings.title: Hold Intent and Management Factors Persist After Canonical Profit Exit
findings.summary: The system successfully exited the second CALL position at 12:10 PM for a +10.0% profit. At the subsequent tick 162, the position data is correctly null, but the intent action remains HOLD with supporting factors for managing the closed position, demonstrating a repeat of the intent state update lag regression.

findings.title: Lagging Intent and Blockers Persist After Position Exit
findings.summary: After the position profit exit at tick 161, the intent state incorrectly persisted as HOLD in tick 162 with factors related to the closed trade. In subsequent ticks including 181, the intent changed to PREPARE_CALL, but continued to rely on a blocker ('No genuinely new evidence since failed CALL') referencing a prior state, indicating persistent latency in intent model updates.

findings.title: Contradictory 'Failed CALL' Blocker After Profitable Exit
findings.summary: The model continues to display latency and inconsistency in assigning post-trade blockers. After profitably exiting the last trade at tick 161, blocker reasoning has fluctuated: tick 197 cited an acceleration re-entry constraint, yet ticks 199 through 202 revert to citing 'No genuinely new evidence since failed CALL.' Referencing a 'failed' call fundamentally contradicts the historical data of the prior profitable execution.

findings.title: Stale Blocker Successfully Overridden for Canonical Entry
findings.summary: The incorrect blocker 'No genuinely new evidence since failed CALL' (noted in ticks 202 to 216) was successfully overridden by the model, clearing the path for the canonical BUY_CALL execution at tick 216. Diagnostic data confirms the override was based on 'fresh local/session frontier' and 'cleared prior attempt extreme' evidence.

findings.title: Hold Intent Persists After Major Vehicle Failure Exit
findings.summary: The position was exited at 1:19 PM via VEHICLE_FAILURE for a -40.0% loss. However, at tick 231 (1:20 PM), the intent action remains HOLD, sourced from 'OPEN_POSITION' with supporting factors still referencing managing the exiting CALL thesis, indicating a critical regression in intent state update latency following an un-planned exit.

findings.title: Reentry Blocked Despite Recognized Override Evidence
findings.summary: The reentry diagnostics at tick 251 explicitly recognize 'cleared prior attempt extreme' as new evidence, yet the intent remains blocked by 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY,' which requires 'material new evidence' for an override. The model fails to implement its own stated logically valid override, demonstrating inconsistent blocker enforcement.

findings.title: Repeated Reentry Block Despite Known Override Evidence
findings.summary: The model continues to block reentry due to 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY,' despite its own diagnostics explicitly recognizing 'cleared prior attempt extreme' as new evidence at tick 271. This demonstrates a repeated failure to apply the logic override when required evidence is present, confirming persistence of the regression identified at tick 251.

findings.title: Persistent Reentry Block Despite Detected Override Evidence
findings.summary: The model continues to block reentry due to 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY', despite explicitly identifying 'cleared prior attempt extreme' as recognized new evidence in its diagnostics at tick 290/291. This persistence confirms the regression where logically valid override criteria are identified but not applied.

findings.title: Trader Rationale Confirms Material New Evidence, Model Fails to Identify Override
findings.summary: The model continues to display the reentry blocker requiring 'material new evidence', despite the trader journal prose explicitly detailing significant new evidence, including violent price breaks correcting prior spikes, weakening local demand, and intensifying negative momentum targeting lower structural areas. This indicates a failure of the AI to objectively translate stated market narrative into structured re-entry evidence for the model.

findings.title: Prose Details Maximal Breakdown Risk, Model Diagnostics See No New Evidence
findings.summary: The model continues to block reentry due to 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY' despite internal journal prose explicitly detailing material new evidence: immediate follow-through to new lows (751.02), intense negative local momentum, and maximal breakdown risk. The AI's structured diagnostics fail to identify this evidence, preventing the required logic override and confirming persistence of the failure identified at tick 311.

findings.title: Prose Confirms Material Breakout Evidence, Diagnostics Persistently Fail to Override Reentry Block
findings.summary: The model persistently blocks reentry via 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY' despite internal journal prose explicitly detailing material new evidence—a violent V-recovery, successful break of prior highs (751.36), and role reversal of resistance to dynamic support. This confirms the persistent failure of AI diagnostics to recognize and map materially new structural evidence to bypass the re-entry blocker, repeating issues seen since tick 251. Additionally, intent state updates show latency, with HOLD persisting immediately after exits.

findings.title: Re-Entry Diagnostics Blind to Continued Bullish Structure, Blocking Valid PREPARE_CALL Intent
findings.summary: The model's re-entry block due to 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY' persists despite objective price action continuation and journal prose detailing a 'consolidation holding above 751.00' and 'intensifying bullish pressure.' At tick 371, price printed 751.4 (spy), clearing prior local highs and validating the structural support mentioned in prose. Internal diagnostics for 'freshFrontier' and 'clearedPriorAttempt' are incorrectly false, demonstrating a recurring failure to logically map obvious market evidence into an override condition. This confirms persistence of the regression identified at tick 251 and repeated most recently at 351.

findings.title: Reentry Blocker Fixed as Diagnostics Recognize Cleared Attempt Extreme
findings.summary: The persistent regression blocking reentry despite new evidence, extensively reported from ticks 251 through 371, is verified as fixed at tick 390. Reentry diagnostics correctly show 'allowed: true' based on the recognized override evidence 'cleared prior attempt extreme.' The current intent block for 'Market not tradeable' is expected, as the 4:00 PM time indicates market close.

findings.title: Initial Session State Shows Expected Warm-Up Blocker
findings.summary: The simulator session started correctly at 9:30 AM. Initial state is set to WAIT with 'Session warming up' listed as the blocker, demonstrating appropriate startup behavior and intent state as expected on tick 1.

findings.title: Canonical PUT Entry Executed on Physical and Accelerator Confirmation
findings.summary: The model executed a canonical BUY_PUT entry at tick 17, filled at $0.28. Internal journal logs confirm that physical price action broke lower, acknowledging the extreme bearish intent (94%) and high accelerator (12.0) present at the time. At tick 18, the position is stable and held for management.

findings.title: Profitable PUT Exit Executed Based on Thesis Change and Price Action
findings.summary: The simulator successfully exited the PUT position at 9:56 AM for a +32.1% profit ($321.43). The AI journal entry accurately cites valid reasons for the exit: the entry thesis (momentum and GEX pressure) had dissolved, and the physical price bounced, negating expectations for immediate continuation.

findings.title: Logical Overrides Properly Trigger Canonical Re-Entry Following Profitable Exit
findings.summary: The simulator properly authorized a new BUY_PUT position at 10:11 AM, filled at $0.28. Internal diagnostics verify that logical overrides correctly cleared previous re-entry blockers. Specifically, 'cleared prior attempt extreme' and 'fresh local/session frontier' were both logically present following the previous profitable exit, allowing the canonical signal to execute accurately as physical price action confirmed aggressive bearish flow.

findings.title: AI Model Timeout Forces Fallback HOLD Decision on Open Position
findings.summary: The current position mindset indicates an 'AI response failure' with the reasoning 'GEMINI_LIVE_TIMEOUT,' resulting in a FALLBACK decision to HOLD. This demonstrates a failure in active model management due to external model latency, preventing fresh tactical assessments for the open position at tick 63.

findings.title: PUT Position Achieves Target Profit for Second Winning Trade
findings.summary: The simulator successfully exited the PUT position at 10:35 AM for a +17.9% profit ($235.97), reaching the target profit criteria. The exit spot was captured at 742.26, successfully achieving the trade's defined objective. This marks the second consecutive profitable PUT exit in the session.

findings.title: Canonical PUT Continuation Correctly Executed After Logical Overrides
findings.summary: The simulator accurately executed another canonical BUY_PUT entry at 10:37 AM, filled at $0.22. Internal diagnostics confirm that logical overrides successfully cleared previous re-entry cooldowns, recognizing fresh bearish continuation evidence in alignment with the multi-window persistence scenario outlined in the journal and MINDSET reasoning.

findings.title: Inaccurate Flat P&L State Reported Despite Active Trade Gain
findings.summary: At Tick 89, the intent state incorrectly reports '+0.0% option P/L' and lists the attribution status as 'flat: true'. The active 736P position was entered at 0.22 and is currently priced at 0.25, representing a 13.6% gain, contradicting the reported diagnostic data.

findings.title: Intent Engine Using Stale Price and Inaccurate P&L Diagnostics
findings.summary: There is a critical data mismatch at Tick 109. The active position legitimately shows the 736P at 0.32 (from 0.22 entry). However, the generated intent state relies on an incorrect contract price of 0.37, derived an inaccurate '+68.2%' P&L, and contradictory reports attribution as 'flat: true'. This indicates the intent model is operating on stale or corrupt price data.

findings.title: PUT Position Exits at Target for Third Profitable Trade
findings.summary: The simulator successfully exited the continuation PUT position at 11:20 AM, achieving a profit of +18.2% ($283.16). The final exit spot of 740.7 accurately satisfied the trade's 'SPOT_TARGET_PROFIT' objective. This constitutes the third successful profitable PUT trade of the session, following the re-entry validated at Tick 69.

findings.title: PUT Reentry for Major Target Extension Properly Authorized and Executed
findings.summary: The simulator properly authorized another standard 'PREPARE_PUT' dynamic reentry at 11:28 AM, capturing the extension below the 740.0 level. Internal diagnostics correctly apply logical overrides for 'new extreme beyond prior episode,' verifying the execution adheres to the tactical reevaluation requirements in the playbook.

findings.title: Extension PUT Exits via Vehicle Failure for Significant Loss
findings.summary: The simulator experienced a 'VEHICLE_FAILURE' exit at 11:37 AM on the extension PUT trade, resulting in a -40.9% ($-753) loss. This occurred as immediate local upward flow generated substantial adverse movement against the continuation thesis. This reflects strategy variance and a failure of the aggressive entry timing in this specific episode, not a simulator bug.

findings.title: AI Narrative Detail Confirms New Evidence, structured Diagnostics Fail to Trigger Reentry Override
findings.summary: The reentry block persists despite the AI journal detailing material new evidence, indicating a failure to map prose narrative into structured diagnostics.

findings.title: Critical Contradiction: Mindset Engine Guides CALL Execution While Intent Remains Bearish For PUT
findings.summary: The system displays radically contradictory intent. The MINDSET engine is actively guiding toward a CALL execution based on bullish narrative, yet the overall Intent state remains PREPARE_PUT with bearish justification, indicating a complete failure of internal logic coherence.

findings.title: Intent Engine Persists with Stale Bearish Factors Despite AI Narrative of Strong Upward Flow
findings.summary: The intent engine displays severe incoherence. It justifies a PREPARE_PUT intent based on 'continuing structure' and claims 'Current leg agrees,' while the AI journal prose simultaneously details active upward flow, re-expanding positive leads, and targeted higher prices, meaning the immediate leg does not agree with a PUT thesis.

findings.title: Intent Engine Ignores Thesis Invalidation Caused by Positive GEX Swap
findings.summary: The Intent Engine persists with PREPARE_PUT, citing "continuing structure" and claiming the "Current leg agrees," despite immediate journal evidence at 12:55 PM explicitly stating that the downside thesis is fully invalidated because the GEX swapped to positive. This indicates a profound disconnect between dynamic structural data and intent generation.

findings.title: Internal Coherence Fixed: Mindset and Intent Align on CALL Action
findings.summary: The critical logic incoherence previously reported, where the Mindset engine guided CALL execution while the Intent persisted with a stale bearish PUT thesis, is verified as fixed. Current intent and mindset states are both aligned on a BUY_CALL action with no blockers, showing restored system cohesion.

findings.title: Structured Diagnostics Fail to Recognize Journaled Momentum Evidence for PUT Reentry Authorization
findings.summary: The dynamic re-entry block persists despite the AI journal explicitly confirming material new evidence. Specifically, the narrative details a "violent break of stasis" and that local leads and price are "synced negatively to the structural bearish mandate," creating a "timing window active." The structured diagnostics fail to recognize this evidence, preventing the logical override for the GEX_VELOCITY blocker.

findings.title: Persistent AI Diagnostic Failure Blocks Authorization of Dynamic PUT Reentry Despite Intense Momentum Narrative
findings.summary: The dynamic re-entry block persists despite repeated, explicit AI journal entries detailing material new evidence. The narrative confirms a "violent resumption of momentum," price "piercing below 745.0," and that price and internal leads are "synced negatively." Crucial structured diagnostics ('frontierThreshold', 'freshFrontier') remain false, failing to map this prose into the logical override required to clear the GEX_VELOCITY block, reproducing the failure seen at tick 249.

findings.title: Intent Engine Claims Leg Agreement Despite AI Recognition of Sluggish Stasis and Timing Failure
findings.summary: The intent engine exhibits severe incoherence between structured factors and recognized narrative. It justifies a PREPARE_PUT action claiming "Current leg agrees," while the simultaneous AI journal entry at 2:17 PM reports that the "Violent trigger through 745.0 is still missing" and that the tactical timing has failed due to sluggishness. The current leg explicitly does not agree with a valid entry thesis.

findings.title: AI Set Arbitrary 'Sluggishness' Threshold, Blocking Valid PUT Reentry Despite Recognized Downside Impulse
findings.summary: The dynamic re-entry block persists despite the AI journal at 2:30 PM detailing a break of stasis to 744.35 and "accel with downside slope." Struct Diagnostics ('clearPriorAttemptExtreme') remain false, failing to map this impulse. Contradictorily, the journal simultaneously calls the move "sluggish" because it didn't "shatter 744.0," setting an arbitrary threshold that overrides playbook criteria and reproduces the failure to authorize reentry seen at ticks 249 and 269.

findings.title: Intent Engine Asserts Leg Agreement Despite AI Narrative Detailing Lack of Momentum
findings.summary: The intent engine reports that 'Current leg agrees' to support PREPARE_PUT, directly contradicting the AI's journaled narrative which explicitly confirms that required momentum was entirely absent and timing risk failed, presenting a severe internal logic mismatch.

findings.title: System Correctly Identifies and Reports low-information Data State
findings.summary: The simulator correctly transitioned into a DATA_LOW_INFORMATION state at 3:17 PM, appropriately triggered by 4 flat SPY ticks, confirming active and correct monitoring of input market data quality.

findings.title: Severe Logic Mismatch: Intent Asserts Leg Agreement Despite AI Narrative Detailing Thesis Failure and Necessary Exit
findings.summary: The intent engine justifies PREPARE_PUT claiming 'Current leg agrees', radically contradicting simultaneous AI journal entries that detail price upticks, extreme pinning risk, and that the thesis is 'dead' requiring 'immediate exit'. This continues the severe logic mismatch regression reported at tick 329.

findings.title: System Correctly Blocked Entry Readiness Near Market Close
findings.summary: At 3:58 PM, the intent action correctly updated to PREPARE_PUT with zero readiness and execution readiness, correctly blocked by 'Market not tradeable.' This correctly reflects system logic disallowing new entries immediately preceding market close.

findings.title: Canonical BUY_CALL Executed Successfully Following Warmup
findings.summary: The system successfully transitioned from warmup and executed a canonical BUY_CALL entry at 9:47 AM (tick 18). Journal prose and mindset align with the structured BUY_CALL intent, confirming correct execution and logic cohesion in the valid opportunity window.

findings.title: Hold Intent Latency After Position Exit via Spot Invalidation
findings.summary: The position was exited via SPOT_INVALIDATION at 9:55 AM. However, at tick 27 (9:56 AM), the intent action incorrectly remains HOLD, sourced from 'OPEN_POSITION', with factors still referencing managing the exited CALL thesis. This indicates persistent latency in intent state update immediately following a structured exit.

findings.title: Valid Re-entry Executed; Diagnostics Correctly Apply Logic Override
findings.summary: The system successfully executed a canonical re-entry BUY_CALL at 10:11 AM. Re-entry diagnostics correctly recognized 'material new evidence' (new extreme, fresh frontier, cleared prior extreme), logically overriding the dynamic GEX_VELOCITY block from the prior loss. This verifies the re-entry logic from prior build regressions is functioning correctly in this scenario.

findings.title: Intent Action 'HOLD' Persists After Major Vehicle Failure Profit Exit
findings.summary: The position was exited via VEHICLE_FAILURE at 10:18 AM for a significant -38.5% loss. However, at tick 50 (10:19 AM), the intent action remains HOLD, sourced from 'OPEN_POSITION', with supporting factors referencing managing the non-existent entry. This reproduces the critical regression of intent state latency following un-planned exits.

findings.title: Reentry Diagnostics Fail to Recognize Cleared extremes with Price Objective Surplus
findings.summary: Reentry is blocked because diagnostics claim "clearedPriorAttempt" and "freshFrontier" are false. However, current SPY price (749.65) objectively exceeds the prior attempt entry spot (749.50) and previous local high (749.55). This indicates a repeated regression where logical override criteria are incorrectly false despite supportive market data, reproducing failures seen in previous builds despite earlier verification of fix at tick 43. Note drawback block is correctly active.

findings.title: Persistence of Invalid Exit-Execution Intent Following Vehicle Failure Exit
findings.summary: AI journal prose explicitly details actively 'executing exit plan' at ticks 87, 89, and 90, contradicting the structured intent action which remains 'PREPARE_CALL' with no current position. This continues the critical latency regression where intent state logic fails to update correctly immediately following non-canonical exits, reproducing issues from prior builds (and earlier in this session at tick 50).

findings.title: Persistent Open-Position Logic Following Vehicle Failure
findings.summary: The system continues to reproduce the critical logic regression where open-position exit management persists after a trade is closed. At tick 110, the AI journal references managing an exit and describes the position as 'highly favorable,' yet the structured position state has been null since the vehicle failure at tick 50 (10:18 AM). This reproduces INTENT_EXIT_EXECUTION_LATENCY_AFTER_VEHICLE_FAILURE_V1_3 reported at tick 90.

findings.title: Session Initializes Correctly in Warmup State
findings.summary: At tick 1 (9:30 AM), the system correctly starts up in a WAIT state, blocked by expected session warming up, confirming initial logic coherence for built v1.3.

findings.title: Canonical BUY_CALL Executed Cohesively
findings.summary: At 9:35 AM (tick 6), the system correctly executed a canonical BUY_CALL entry. Mindset, journal prose, and structured execution state were all cohesive, verifying the fix for prior logic disconnects and standard entry operation.

findings.title: Intent State Latency Fix Verified Post-Exit
findings.summary: Following the thesis exit at tick 9, the intent state correctly and immediately transitioned from HOLD to WAIT at tick 10 (9:39 AM), with readiness resetting to zero and the expected post-exit blocker applied. This verifies the fix for the critical open-position logic latency regression seen in prior builds and earlier in this build sequence.

findings.title: Re-entry Logic Override Fix Verified
findings.summary: The system successfully executed a canonical re-entry BUY_CALL at 9:57 AM (tick 28). Re-entry diagnostics correctly mapped market data to recognize 'cleared prior attempt extreme,' overriding the dynamic block from the prior loss at tick 9. This verifies the fix for reentry logic regressions where logical override criteria were identified but not applied.

findings.title: Post-Exit Intent Latency Fix Verified Repeatedly
findings.summary: Following the position exit via VEHICLE_FAILURE at tick 40, the intent state correctly and immediately transitioned from HOLD to WAIT at tick 41 (10:10 AM). The readiness reset to zero and the correct post-exit blocker were applied. This confirms the verified fix for open-position logic latency following non-canonical exits, reproducing the successful reset seen at tick 10.

findings.title: AI Narrative Describes High Conviction Logic Override, Diagnostics Fails to Update Reentry State
findings.summary: The AI journal explicitly details material new evidence and a 'high-conviction setup forming' for a BUY_CALL, yet the structured reentry diagnostics remain blocked by 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY' with no stated evidence. This indicates a critical breakdown and regression where the model fails to apply its logical override criteria based on recognized narrative, repeating failures reported in previous build sequences.

findings.title: Intent Engine Reports 'Current Leg Agrees' Despite AI Narrative Detailing Stasis and Lack of Momentum
findings.summary: The intent engine's structured diagnostic cites 'Current leg agrees' as a supporting factor for PREPARE_CALL, directly contradicting simultaneous AI journal prose which describes a continuation of 'quiet accumulation' with 'localized tension easing' and 'immediate breakout momentum...not yet re-activated'. This demonstrates incoherence between the narrative and structured intent criteria.

findings.title: Fix Verified: Reentry Logic Override Function Properly Dynamic and Cohesive with AI Narrative
findings.summary: The reentry logic override function is verified as fixed. At tick 101, the AI journal details an imminent explosive upward resolution, and structured reentry diagnostics cohesively match this narrative by showing 'allowed: true' based on recognized material new evidence ('new extreme beyond prior episode'). This verifies that logical override criteria are correctly identified and applied.

findings.title: Fix Verified: Reentry Override Logic Remains Stable and Coherent Through Continuation
findings.summary: The fix for the reentry logic override function is confirmed as stable and coherent. At tick 121, both the AI journal narrative and structured reentry diagnostics properly recognize material new evidence to maintain an 'allowed' status, despite dynamic blockers like drawdown review being present. This demonstrates sustained logical consistency.

findings.title: System Correctly Identifies Data Staleness and Applies Intent Blocker
findings.summary: Data staleness detection and blocking is verified as functional. At tick 141, market data age exceeds the threshold (10-11 seconds), and the system correctly transitions Data Health to 'DATA_STALE_OR_NONINFORMATIVE', logs this in the AI journal, and applies the corresponding blocker to PREPARE_CALL intent.

findings.title: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' While AI Narrative Details Violent Counter-Directional Compression
findings.summary: The intent engine continues to report 'Current leg agrees' as a supporting factor for PREPARE_CALL, despite simultaneous AI journal prose detailing violent negative compression builds (-0.59). This confirms persistence of the logic breakdown where structured criteria become disjointed from the recognized market narrative.

findings.title: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' While AI Narrative Describes Stationary Price and Normalizing Negative Tension
findings.summary: The intent engine still asserts 'Current leg agrees' to support PREPARE_CALL, despite simultaneous AI journal prose explicitly describing price as 'stationary' and localized tension as in 'minor normalization (-0.62)'. This confirms that the logic linkage reported as incoherent at ticks 81 and 161 remains broken in this build.

findings.title: Intent Engine Asserts 'Leg Agreement' Contradicting AI Narrative of Local Drifting
findings.summary: The structured intent engine claims 'Current leg agrees' as a supporting factor for PREPARE_CALL. This directly contradicts simultaneous AI journal narrative explicitly describing price as having 'drifted minimally' lower and immediate market moves being slightly negative, indicating the local leg is not actively agreeing with the CALL direction. This confirms persistence of the logic breakdown reported at ticks 81, 161, and 181.

findings.title: Fix Verified: Reentry Logic Remains Stable and Coherent Under Drawdown Review Blocker
findings.summary: The fix for reentry override logic stability is confirmed as stable at tick 221. Despite the presence of a dynamic blocker ('DRAWDOWN_REVIEW_REQUIRES_MODEL_CHANGE') resulting from the prior trade loss, the system's re-entry diagnostics consistently identify material new evidence ('cleared prior attempt extreme'). findings.title: Fix Verified: Intent Engine 'Price Persistence' Factor Coheres with Breakout Narrative
findings.summary: Fix Verified: The structured intent engine's supporting factor 'Price persistence' is verified as coherent with the recognized narrative at tick 241. AI journal prose explicitly describes price as 'holding 7545' after an 'initiated breakout', which is directly supported by transmission data showing positive movement. This confirms the system correctly links structured criteria to narrative evidence, resolving prior incoherence.

findings.title: Structured Intent Criteria Contain Logical Contradiction Between Supporting Factors and Gaps
findings.summary: A logical contradiction exists within the structured data at tick 261. 'Price persistence' is simultaneously listed as an active 'Gap' (preventing execution) and as NOT listed in the 'SupportingFactors' (consistent), however comparing to tick 241, where it was verified as fixed, it seems the factors logic is inconsistent. Furthermore, previous bug reports (ticks 161, 181, 201) regarding 'Current leg agrees' persist, indicating disjointed structured logic generation. Therefore, the highest value observation is the internal contradiction in data structure between supporting factors and gaps definitions.

findings.title: Persistent Contradiction: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' During Price Stasis

Price stasis also confirms the persistence of the 'Price persistence' gap. findings.title: Fix Verified Stability: Reentry Diagnostics Dynamically Map Price Extension to Override Material Evidence

findings.title: Persistent Contradiction: Intent Engine Claims CALL 'Leg Agreement' During Price Retracement
findings.summary: At tick 361, the structured intent engine cites 'Current leg agrees' as a supporting factor for the CALL direction. findings.title: Persistent Incoherence: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' Despite Contradictory Price Movement

findings.title: Simulator Session Starts Successfully with Expected Warming Up State
findings.summary: At tick 1 (9:30 AM), the simulator successfully initiated in WAIT mode with the expected 'Session warming up' blocker. No previous version context applies as this is the start of a new playback session.

findings.title: Persistent Issue: Bullish 'Leg Agreement' Factor Contradicts Active Guidance for PUT and Bearish Narrative
findings.summary: The structured Intent engine incorrectly cites 'Current leg agrees' at tick 21.
